content.text: An LLM system has four independent versioning axes: the model weights (or provider-hosted model identifier), the prompt configuration, the retrieval index and tool registry, and the application code. Each axis changes at a different cadence, for different reasons, and with different rollback requirements. Treating the system as a single versioned artefact obscures which axis caused a quality regression; treating each axis as completely independent makes it impossible to reproduce a specific combination in staging.
— a human-readable label that captures the pinned value of every axis — stored in a single configuration file that is committed to Git and deployed as an artefact alongside the application.

attrs.alt: LLM versioning axes: Model Weights, Prompt Config, RAG/Tools, App — each with ID scheme, pin location, change trigger, and cadence

content.text: Pin all four axes in a single

file that is committed to Git and read at application startup. This file is the single source of truth — no model ID should ever be hardcoded in application code. The file travels with the container image; deploying a different image version automatically changes the configuration it carries.

# config/models.yaml
# Single source of truth for all model identifiers.
# Every environment reads from this file — never hardcode model IDs.

models:
  primary:
    provider: anthropic
    model_id: claude-sonnet-4-6
    max_tokens: 4096
    temperature: 0.0
  canary:
    provider: anthropic
    model_id: claude-sonnet-4-6   # updated during canary deploy
    max_tokens: 4096
    temperature: 0.0
  judge:
    provider: anthropic
    model_id: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
    max_tokens: 256
    temperature: 0.0

prompts:
  system_prompt: prompts/v2.3.1/system.txt
  rag_template:  prompts/v2.3.1/rag_context.txt

infrastructure:
  vector_index_version: 3
  tool_registry_version: 1.2
Store prompt templates in a versioned subdirectory (

) rather than inline in the YAML file. This allows diffs between versions to be read as normal text diffs and allows the prompt directory to be audited independently from the rest of the configuration.
environment variable. Log the composite label on every startup and attach it to every request in the
table. This makes it possible to segment any metric — latency, feedback rate, quality score — by the exact version combination that was active when the request was served.

# app/version.py
"""Build and validate the composite system version at startup."""
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class SystemVersion:
    model_id: str
    prompt_version: str
    index_version: int
    tool_version: str
    app_version: str    # from APP_VERSION env var or git tag

    def label(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"model={self.model_id} "
            f"prompt={self.prompt_version} "
            f"index=v{self.index_version} "
            f"app={self.app_version}"
        )

def load_system_version() -> SystemVersion:
    cfg = yaml.safe_load(Path('config/models.yaml').read_text())
    import os
    return SystemVersion(
        model_id=cfg['models']['primary']['model_id'],
        prompt_version=Path(cfg['prompts']['system_prompt']).parts[1],
        index_version=cfg['infrastructure']['vector_index_version'],
        tool_version=str(cfg['infrastructure']['tool_registry_version']),
        app_version=os.environ.get('APP_VERSION', 'dev'),
    )

# Log at startup
SYSTEM_VERSION = load_system_version()
print(f'System version: {SYSTEM_VERSION.label()}')

Attach the composite version to every row in the request log table. Without this, a quality regression detected in an aggregated dashboard cannot be traced back to the version that caused it — you know something went wrong but not when it was introduced.

-- Add version columns to the request log table
ALTER TABLE request_log ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
    model_id       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    prompt_version TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    index_version  INT  NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    app_version    TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';

-- Query: compare quality metrics by prompt version
SELECT
    prompt_version,
    COUNT(*) AS requests,
    AVG(CASE WHEN signal = 'thumbs_up' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)   AS pos_rate,
    AVG(CASE WHEN signal = 'thumbs_down' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS neg_rate,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY latency_ms) / 1000.0 AS p95_s
FROM request_log
WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
GROUP BY prompt_version
ORDER BY prompt_version;

The query above lets you spot when a prompt version or model change shifted the negative feedback rate. Keep the version columns indexed so queries over rolling 7-day windows remain fast even at millions of rows per day.

Create a Git tag for every composite release. The tag embeds the values of all four axes so the exact combination can be reproduced from the tag alone, without reading the flag store or querying a database.

#!/bin/bash
# scripts/tag_release.sh
# Create a composite release tag that captures all version axes.

set -euo pipefail

MODEL_ID=$(python -c "
import yaml; c=yaml.safe_load(open('config/models.yaml'))
print(c['models']['primary']['model_id'])")

PROMPT_V=$(python -c "
import yaml; from pathlib import Path
c=yaml.safe_load(open('config/models.yaml'))
print(Path(c['prompts']['system_prompt']).parts[1])")

APP_V=${APP_VERSION:-$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)}

TAG="release/${APP_V}__model-${MODEL_ID}__prompt-${PROMPT_V}"

git tag -a "$TAG" -m "Composite release: $TAG"
git push origin "$TAG"
echo "Tagged: $TAG"
Tags are immutable references in Git. A tag like
is self-documenting: an on-call engineer can read a tag from an incident report, check out that exact state, and reproduce the failing request without any external lookup.
Prompt changes are the most frequent version changes. Advancing the prompt version should be a scripted operation — not a manual edit — to prevent partial updates (updating
) and to enforce that the new directory exists before the config is modified.

# scripts/migrate_prompt_version.py
"""Safely advance the system prompt version in config/models.yaml."""
import yaml, shutil
from pathlib import Path

CONFIG = Path('config/models.yaml')
PROMPTS_DIR = Path('prompts')

def advance_prompt(new_version: str) -> None:
    # Validate new version directory exists
    new_dir = PROMPTS_DIR / new_version
    assert new_dir.is_dir(), f'Prompt dir not found: {new_dir}'
    assert (new_dir / 'system.txt').exists(), 'system.txt missing'
    assert (new_dir / 'rag_context.txt').exists(), 'rag_context.txt missing'

    # Update config
    cfg = yaml.safe_load(CONFIG.read_text())
    old_version = Path(cfg['prompts']['system_prompt']).parts[1]
    cfg['prompts']['system_prompt'] = f'prompts/{new_version}/system.txt'
    cfg['prompts']['rag_template']  = f'prompts/{new_version}/rag_context.txt'
    CONFIG.write_text(yaml.dump(cfg, default_flow_style=False))
    print(f'Prompt version: {old_version} -> {new_version}')
    print('Review config/models.yaml and commit the change.')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    import sys
    advance_prompt(sys.argv[1])  # e.g. v2.4.0
and commit it. The CI pipeline's prompt test suite (snapshot tests + LLM-judge) runs automatically on the commit and catches regressions before deployment.

Not all axis combinations are compatible. Document the compatibility matrix explicitly so engineers do not accidentally pair a model version with a prompt version that was written for a different model:
— a prompt that relies on extended thinking or tool-use syntax specific to Claude must not be paired with a different provider's model. Track
— changing the vector index schema (e.g. adding a metadata field) requires a corresponding app change. Version them together with a shared minor version bump.
— if the prompt references a tool by name, changing the tool's name in the registry breaks the prompt. Treat tool-name changes as breaking changes and bump the tool registry major version.

Pin all versions in staging to match production before running end-to-end tests. A staging environment that uses a different model ID than production will not catch model-specific regressions before they reach users.

Not all version changes are safe to deploy automatically. A model change or a prompt major version bump requires the full eval suite to pass before deployment. Automate the detection of these breaking changes in a PR check so engineers are warned before they merge.

# scripts/check_breaking_changes.py
"""Detect breaking version-axis changes before a deploy."""
import yaml, sys
from pathlib import Path

def load_config(path: str) -> dict:
    return yaml.safe_load(Path(path).read_text())

def check_breaking(old: dict, new: dict) -> list[str]:
    issues = []

    # Model change
    old_model = old['models']['primary']['model_id']
    new_model = new['models']['primary']['model_id']
    if old_model != new_model:
        issues.append(f'MODEL CHANGED: {old_model} -> {new_model} (requires eval gate)')

    # Prompt major version bump
    from pathlib import PurePosixPath
    old_pv = PurePosixPath(old['prompts']['system_prompt']).parts[1]  # e.g. v2.3.1
    new_pv = PurePosixPath(new['prompts']['system_prompt']).parts[1]
    old_major = int(old_pv.split('.')[0][1:])
    new_major = int(new_pv.split('.')[0][1:])
    if new_major > old_major:
        issues.append(f'PROMPT MAJOR BUMP: {old_pv} -> {new_pv} (run full eval suite)')

    # Index version change
    old_idx = old['infrastructure']['vector_index_version']
    new_idx = new['infrastructure']['vector_index_version']
    if new_idx != old_idx:
        issues.append(f'INDEX CHANGED: v{old_idx} -> v{new_idx} (re-run RAG recall tests)')

    return issues

if __name__ == '__main__':
    old = load_config(sys.argv[1])  # config from HEAD~1
    new = load_config(sys.argv[2])  # config from HEAD
    issues = check_breaking(old, new)
    for issue in issues:
        print(f'WARNING: {issue}')
    # Return exit code 1 if breaking changes require manual approval
    breaking = [i for i in issues if 'MODEL CHANGED' in i or 'MAJOR BUMP' in i]
    sys.exit(1 if breaking else 0)

# .github/workflows/version-check.yml
name: Version Compatibility Check

on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'config/models.yaml'
      - 'prompts/**'

jobs:
  breaking-change-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { fetch-depth: 2 }
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install pyyaml
      - run: |
          git show HEAD~1:config/models.yaml > /tmp/old_config.yaml
          python scripts/check_breaking_changes.py \
            /tmp/old_config.yaml config/models.yaml

  prompt-snapshot:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: contains(github.event.pull_request.changed_files, 'prompts/')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install -r requirements-test.txt
      - env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: pytest tests/prompts/ -v --tb=short
The breaking-change check runs on every PR that modifies
directory. A model ID change or prompt major version bump returns exit code 1, blocking merge until the eval suite passes. Minor prompt version bumps (patch changes, typo fixes) pass automatically.
The Git release tag captures the version state at deploy time, but querying Git tags during an incident is slow. Maintain a
table in the operational database so you can answer "what version was running at 14:32 UTC on June 15?" with a single SQL query during an incident.

-- Track every deployed version combination
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS version_registry (
    id             SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    deployed_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    model_id       TEXT NOT NULL,
    prompt_version TEXT NOT NULL,
    index_version  INT  NOT NULL,
    app_version    TEXT NOT NULL,
    deployed_by    TEXT,          -- GitHub Actions run ID or user
    git_tag        TEXT,          -- composite release tag
    notes          TEXT
);

-- Insert on every deploy (called from CI)
INSERT INTO version_registry
    (model_id, prompt_version, index_version, app_version, deployed_by, git_tag)
VALUES
    ('claude-sonnet-4-6', 'v2.3.1', 3, 'v4.7.2',
     'github-actions/run-12345',
     'release/v4.7.2__model-claude-sonnet-4-6__prompt-v2.3.1');

-- Query: which version was live during an incident window?
SELECT * FROM version_registry
WHERE deployed_at <= '2026-06-15 14:30:00+00'
ORDER BY deployed_at DESC
LIMIT 1;
Insert a row into this table on every deploy from CI. The
timestamp plus a simple ordered query lets you pinpoint which version combination was active at the moment a user reported an issue. Cross-reference with
to see exactly which requests were served by which version.
Adopt a three-part version number for prompt directories:
. Each part has a defined meaning in the context of prompt changes:
Restructure changes expected output format or requires model change
Switch from XML tags to JSON output
Add few-shot examples; new instruction section
Non-functional edit; no expected output change

Major bumps require the same approval process as a model change: run the full eval suite, advance through a canary, and create a composite release tag. Patch bumps can be deployed automatically if all snapshot tests pass.
Hardcoded model IDs in application code
— a model ID buried in a Python file will not be picked up by the breaking-change checker and will not appear in the composite version label. Enforce the rule that all model IDs must come from
via a lint rule or grep in CI.

than production, end-to-end tests in staging do not validate the production configuration. Deploy the same config file to both environments; use environment variables only for credentials, not model identifiers.
No version on the request log
— without stamping requests with the active version, you cannot segment quality metrics by version change. Adding this column retroactively to millions of rows is painful; add it before you need it.
in place after it has been deployed destroys the ability to reproduce historical requests. Treat deployed prompt directories as immutable; always create a new version directory.
LLM systems have four independent versioning axes (model weights, prompt config, RAG/tools, app code), each changing at a different cadence. Pin all four in a single

committed to Git; read it at startup to build a composite version label; stamp every request with that label for segmented metric analysis. Create Git release tags that embed all axis values so any production state can be reproduced from the tag alone. Advance prompt versions through a script that validates directory structure before modifying the config file.
